name: Guadiana Natural Way: Corral de Calatrava Loop
name_original: Camino natural del Río Guadiana — circular por el Corral de Calatrava
description: Hike the moderate 7.1-mile Guadiana Natural Way: Corral de Calatrava Loop, exploring river views, fields, and the historic Geldres Mill.
region_intro: Hiking around Corral De Calatrava offers diverse landscapes shaped by volcanic activity and river systems. The region features extinct volcanoes, such as Cabezo del Rey, providing unique geological formations. The Guadiana and Jabalón rivers traverse the area, contributing to varied riverine environments. Trails often cross the Castilian plateau, characterized by crop fields, small forests, and scrubland. Best hiking trails around Corral De Calatrava The most popular hiking route is Route of the Battle of Alarcos — circular from Poblete, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical sites and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail highlights the region's unique volcanic landscapes and picturesque lagoons. Local hikers also love the Guadiana Natural Way: Corral de Calatrava Loop, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past historic features, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Corral De Calatrava is defined by volcanic terrain, river systems, and the Castilian plateau. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate hikes. The routes in Corral De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 57 hikers have used komoot to explore Corral De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Corral de Calatrava?
A: Corral de Calatrava offers a selection of 17 hiking trails, catering to various preferences. The majority, 14 routes, are considered moderate, while 3 are easy, making the area accessible for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Corral de Calatrava?
A: Yes, Corral de Calatrava has 3 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes allow you to enjoy the region's natural beauty without excessive effort, often featuring gentle ascents and descents across the Castilian plateau.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Corral de Calatrava?
A: Hiking in Corral de Calatrava offers diverse landscapes. You'll encounter unique volcanic terrain, including extinct volcanoes like Cabezo del Rey, and river systems such as the Guadiana and Jabalón. Many trails also traverse the typical Castilian plateau, characterized by crop fields, small forests, and scrubland.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Corral de Calatrava?
A: Yes, many routes in Corral de Calatrava are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198486760/route-of-the-battle-of-alarcos-circular-from-poblete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Battle of Alarcos — circular from Poblete&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.2 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a 10 km circular experience through volcanic landscapes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on the trails?
A: Corral de Calatrava's trails often lead to significant historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3882572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt;, an important site with remains from various historical periods, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage and Maares Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; atop Cabezo del Rey. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198481191/ascent-to-castillo-de-caracuel-de-calatrava-circular-from-corral-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Castillo de Caracuel de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; also offers a chance to explore local heritage.

Q: Are there any trails that feature rivers or water bodies?
A: Absolutely. The region is shaped by the Guadiana and Jabalón rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197967208/guadiana-natural-way-corral-de-calatrava-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Way: Corral de Calatrava Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for river views and passes by the historic Geldres Mill. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old dam of the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Corral de Calatrava?
A: The trails in Corral de Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 9 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the unique volcanic features, and the well-maintained paths that offer a blend of natural beauty and historical interest.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic scenery along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, several trails offer stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maares Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, located at the top of Cabezo del Rey, provides expansive vistas of the volcanic landscape. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another spot to enjoy scenic outlooks.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the volcanic features of the region?
A: Corral de Calatrava is known for its volcanic terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to highlight the region's unique volcanic landscapes and picturesque lagoons. You can also find extinct volcanoes like Cabezo del Rey along various routes.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Corral de Calatrava?
A: Moderate hikes in Corral de Calatrava typically range from 2.5 to 3.5 hours. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198486760/route-of-the-battle-of-alarcos-circular-from-poblete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Battle of Alarcos — circular from Poblete&lt;/a&gt; takes about 2 hours 27 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197967208/guadiana-natural-way-corral-de-calatrava-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Way: Corral de Calatrava Loop&lt;/a&gt; is usually completed in around 2 hours 59 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to interesting natural formations like caves?
A: Yes, for those interested in natural formations, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5047946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Shepherd's Cave&lt;/a&gt;. This highlight offers an opportunity to explore a unique geological feature within the region's diverse landscape.
tips: Church dedicated to the patron saint of Corral de Calatrava, Our Lady of Peace. It was built in the 18th century, with a Latin cross plan with a single nave, with a dome over the transept, with little developed arms and a polygonal head.
A place worth visiting, where the Guadiana flow can be well appreciated.

You can hardly appreciate it, because it is difficult to get close by the undergrowth and there is almost nothing left, a pity that we do not take care of our historical heritage. But hey, at least we have the dam just 200 meters away and it is worth visiting.
It is a Gothic-Mudejar building, probably from the late fifteenth or early sixteenth century.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Guadiana Natural Way: Corral de Calatrava Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact loop are not detailed, Corral de Calatrava is a key point along the larger Guadiana Natural Way (GR 114). You can typically find parking within or near the town of Corral de Calatrava to access sections of this route. The trail often follows easily accessible paths and tracks.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally suitable for beginners and families. The overall Guadiana Natural Way, of which this loop is a part, often features forest tracks and easily accessible paths with minimal elevation changes. The difficulty for this specific loop is rated as moderate, but many sections are not exceptionally challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain along this route traverses the fields of Calatrava, often running alongside the Guadiana River. You can expect a mix of pastures, cultivated fields (including olive groves), and areas rich in diverse flora. Sections may include good dirt tracks, and sometimes old, narrow, asphalted paths. The landscape combines natural riverine environments with agricultural zones.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience the scenic Guadiana River and its surrounding ecosystems. You might also encounter historical sites like the Geldres Mill, and religious buildings such as the Hermitage of Our Lady of Peace and Our Lady of the Annunciation. The trail also passes through protected natural areas, offering panoramic views of the river and surrounding plains.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route traverses protected natural spaces, including a Site of Community Importance (SCI) and Special Protection Area for Birds (SPA). This means you have a good chance of spotting diverse bird species, including royal herons and white storks. The area is also a critical habitat for species like the Iberian Imperial Eagle and Black Stork, and the Guadiana basin is known for its rich flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Is the Guadiana Natural Way: Corral de Calatrava Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through cultivated fields and protected natural areas, it's essential to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and respect agricultural land. Always ensure your dog is under control and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this route. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot in this region, making hiking less comfortable, while winters can be cooler but still pleasant on sunny days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike this route. It is part of the public access Guadiana Natural Way, which is freely accessible to hikers.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the much larger Camino Natural del Guadiana, also known as the GR 114, which stretches over 850 kilometers. The loop around Corral de Calatrava covers sections of this long-distance trail, specifically marking the end of Stage 10 and the beginning of Stage 11 of the GR 114.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, the town of Corral de Calatrava, where the loop is centered, offers basic amenities such as cafes and possibly restrooms. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially when venturing into more remote sections of the route.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Peace
